The losses in this game dont feel fair or genuine (cause you dont know what you did wrong) and on top of the frustrating gameplay and tedious talking (when you are on your 7th-10th attempt in a row, it gets old)...it just doesnt make you want to try again.

The animatronics are well designed and Baby's voice is creepy as hell but thats pretty much it for me.

I can see what he was going for here though.

[h1]🤖 FNAF: Sister Location — Линейный хоррор-эволюция[/h1]

Five Nights at Freddy's: Sister Location — [u]самый инновационный[/u] FNAF. 🏭 Если FNAF 4 был амашем на пиццерию из первой части, то здесь — полноценный сюжетный хоррор с линейными миссиями. Новые механики, говорящие аниматроники, кульминация лора. Шаг вперёд для серии! 🎤😱

[h2]🚶‍♂️ Линейные ночи: От статичного выживания к приключениям[/h2]
Геймплей кардинально новый. Вместо камер — [u]активные миссии[/u]: дойди до электрощитовой, восстанови питание, отключи систему. Каждая ночь — отдельная глава с целями. 

Например, в первой ночи крадёшься по коридорам, слушаешь шаги Circus Baby, прячешься. Динамика вместо статики — [b]освежает[/b] серию! 🏃‍♂️🔧

[h2]🔊 Говорящие аниматроники: Лор оживает[/h2]
Аниматроники [u]разговаривают[/u]! Circus Baby, Ballora, Funtime Foxy, Ennard — у каждого характер. Baby рассказывает что здесь происходит и создает доверие ("Я твой друг"), Ballora танцует под музыку, Funtime Freddy шутит и угрожает. 

Диалоги раскрывают лор: эксперименты, души детей, предательства. Это [u]прорыв[/u] — монстры обрели голоса и личности. 🎭

[h2]⚠️ Минус: Рестарты раздражают[/h2]
Единственный [u]недостаток[/u] — провал миссии = перепроход всей зоны. Сдох от Фредди в щитовой? Иди сначала через коридоры с Ballora'ой. Раздражает, особенно на поздних ночах. 

Хотелось бы чекпоинты. Но это учит осторожности — типично FNAF. 😤🔄

[h2]🎪 Атмосфера и новые механики[/h2]
Локации подземного бункера — [u]лабиринт ужасов[/u]. Стеклянные коридоры, бальный зал, театр. Звуковой дизайн: музыка Ballora, смех Foxy, шёпот Ennard — паранойя на максимуме. 

Механики разнообразны: лазер-таги, хакерство, рукопожатия с Funtime Freddy. Каждая ночь уникальна. Финал — [b]кульминация[/b]! 🎪🔦

[h2]🏁 Вердикт: Прорыв в серии FNAF[/h2]
Sister Location берёт лучшее от прошлых частей и добавляет сюжет, персонажей, динамику. Говорящие роботы и линейные миссии — свежо и страшно. Минус рестартов прощается эмоциями.

[b]Играйте, если:[/b]
[list]
[*] Прошли FNAF 1-4 и хотите эволюции
[*] Любите сюжетный хоррор с персонажами
[*] Фанатеете по лору FNAF
[/list]

[b]Пропустите только если:[/b]
[list]
[*] Не любите перепроходить секции
[/list]

[quote]От камер к приключениям — Sister Location меняет правила FNAF.[/quote]

[h1]Оценка: 9/10 🎪🤖[/h1]

222945559.review:
This game is vastly different from the previous 4 and I think that’s great. Firstly, instead of the story being told through cryptic messages, newspapers or background information, you are getting the story front and center this time around. This game takes a different approach after a complaint to the previous games story being quite confusing so here we get a cohesive story with still some Fnaf charm to it. Secondly, the gameplay just like the 4th game is different from the original 3 with this one allowing you to slightly move throughout the environment and instead of looking at cameras (maybe, there is a secret place) or surviving in a bedroom you will be provided certain tasks to complete the night. And thirdly, there is fully voice acted characters in this game now other than the phone guy from the previous and it is done phenomenally. I will say the scares here depending on the area and animation are either gonna make you jump or will get no reaction out of you. However the environments and sound design may also be able to provide the scares you may crave. 

This game tends to have the reputation of being peoples least favorite of the series and providing not a great experience especially when it first released. Now while this is the case and those criticisms are valid, I share the hot take of this being my favorite Fnaf game of the original 6 games. I love how different this game is from the rest, I love the music despite how little there is, love the story being put upfront, and I love the characters introduced here and even some became staples of the series. I of course recommend this game and I hope you can see what I see in this game.

216427647.review:
[h2]Unnecessarily Complicated Five Nights at Freddy's: Sister Location Review[/h2]

[b]TL;DR:[/b] This one is quite different from the first 4. It is fun to play for the first time because each night has a unique gameplay. However, replaying the game is not fun at all because it is all scripted, there is no variation whatsoever. Paradoxically, the most fun mode is the custom night. Overall Score: 78/100 - Comfortably Recommend.

[h3]Unnecessarily Long Version with 5 Categories.[/h3]

[b]Cost per Hour:[/b] $8 for ~13 hours of gameplay ($0.62/h). This playtime includes completing the main 5 nights twice, completing all custom night challenges and [spoiler]Golden Freddy[/spoiler] mode twice. That is more than all other games (primarily because of the custom night challenges). Category Score: 9/10

[b]Gameplay:[/b] This game doesn’t have a “standard” gameplay loop like the previous ones but there are some things that will repeat every night. Checking the lights and climbing the vents. Since this game is narrative-focused, it is interesting to hear the dialogues during the first playthrough but, with no option to skip it, you’ll have to listen to it every single time, if you ever want to replay this game. Night 1 is a tutorial and night 2 involves probably my second favorite section in the game ([spoiler]Ballora + Freddy. Both are predictable but fun to play[/spoiler]). Night 3 has an interesting repair concept that will be better executed in Five Nights at Freddy's: Help Wanted. Night 4 is a nightmare. I had to script it out on my phone by seconds  to beat it. Night 5 is simultaneously the worst and the best. [spoiler]Ennard section is one of the best executions of a classical FNaF experience in the series. You HAVE to use the cameras but sound is as important[/spoiler]. While the main game is not [i]that[/i] great, the custom night is the best in the series. Very well balanced, basically no RNG involved, pure skill. Category Score: 7/10

[b]Visuals:[/b] While quality wise there are no issues, if you didn’t notice the fish-eye effect in the previous games, you [i]will[/i] notice it here, especially during the night 5 repair. It doesn’t ruin the game by any means but this was the first game where I realized that this was how this game works. A lot of different locations, a lot of moving parts, and jumpscare animations are great. Category Score: 8/10

[b]Sound design:[/b] The sound design is more about terror than horror, if that makes sense. Can’t say that it is bad, I think that it builds up the horror during the game. Music, though, is very fitting and tense. Category Score: 7/10

[b]Technical performance / Compatibility:[/b] It’s bad… Someone decided that a custom cursor is an amazing idea that absolutely won’t break quite literally everything. Capturing this game can also be a challenge but you should have no problems if you are using OBS. Linux performance differs from person to person. I used Proton-GE 10-28 and found zero issues. Some players, though, report sound issues, fps issues, input lag issues, etc. Once again, as usual, ProtonDB is your best friend for Linux gaming. Category Score: 8/10

This gives a total of 39/50 (78%). This game is a miss for me, honestly. While I like the novelty of gameplay and the old format was getting stale 4 games in, the execution is just not great. BUT, it is not absolutely horrible either.

215001756.review:
[h1] The Most Underrated FNAF in the Franchise [/h1]

In my opinion, this is the most underrated part of the whole old Five Nights at Freddy's era. Back then, I couldn’t get past the second night with Funtime Freddy, but even that was enough to see that the game is way ahead of many main entries.

The best part is that the gameplay finally stepped out of the cramped security office. We’re not just staring at cameras and pressing buttons; we keep moving between locations and doing really varied, intense tasks. There are secrets everywhere and multiple ways to get through the game, which felt like a real breath of fresh air for the series. Scott may have thrown in some awkward jokes, but he gave players a lot more freedom without turning the gameplay into a dull routine.

Despite all this "pink" aesthetic and the "dressed-up" animatronics, the game knows how to be scary. The local robots (well, except maybe Bon Bon) look creepy and can still give you a fright when you least expect it. The atmosphere is just as heavy as in the gloomy corridors of the previous installments.

Back in 2016, a lot of people complained that the game had become too easy due to checkpoints and the way it basically held the player’s hand, guiding every move. But EVEN BY FNAF STANDARDS, it was a huge step forward for the series. If you skipped or didn’t like Five Nights at Freddy's: Sister Location back then, now’s the perfect time to revisit it, replay it, and see it in a new light.

[h3] Verdict: [/h3] https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3628292546 

[i] P. S. 2x. Did you like the review or my profile? Drop a Steam reward for the review. Thanks to your support, I don't lose the point of playing and writing reviews! [/i]

203087023.review:
Five Nights at Freddy’s: Sister Location takes a bold turn from the formula established in the earlier entries of the FNaF series. While still very much rooted in the survival horror genre, this fifth main installment introduces a new environment, a stronger narrative focus, and significant gameplay variety that sets it apart from the static, camera-focused tension of the first four games. Developed by Scott Cawthon and released on Steam, Sister Location is more than just a continuation—it’s a reimagining of what a Five Nights game can be, for better or worse.

Unlike previous games where the player is stuck in a single room monitoring cameras and closing doors, Sister Location provides a more dynamic and interactive experience. You play as Michael Afton, a technician working at the underground facility known as Circus Baby’s Entertainment and Rentals. Each night presents a new set of tasks in different rooms, from crawling through dark maintenance tunnels to manually resetting animatronics or handling faulty systems under intense pressure. The environments are more varied, and the objectives differ with each shift, giving the game an episodic feel rather than a strictly looping cycle of survival.

What sets Sister Location apart is its willingness to weave a more direct and cinematic narrative into the gameplay. Voice acting plays a prominent role here, especially through the sardonic AI guide known as HandUnit, who delivers deadpan instructions that are often misleading or ironic. The story gradually unfolds through cutscenes, audio recordings, and hidden lore, diving deeper into the twisted history behind the animatronics, the Afton family, and the dark underbelly of the Fazbear universe. The plot leans into themes of control, deception, and identity, with twists that are not only unsettling but also emotionally impactful for those familiar with the broader FNaF lore.

Mechanically, the game constantly changes its approach, which can be both refreshing and disorienting. Each night introduces new systems: managing oxygen while hiding in a closet, fending off Ballora with sound cues, or carefully shocking Circus Baby based on her behavior. The unpredictability keeps players on edge, but it also creates uneven difficulty spikes. Some nights feel polished and well-balanced, while others rely on trial and error or frustratingly precise execution. The lack of consistent mechanics may alienate players who preferred the tighter loop of the earlier games, though it undeniably adds variety and unpredictability to the horror experience.

Sister Location also introduces more overtly theatrical elements than its predecessors. There are voice-acted cutscenes, a satirical soap opera titled The Immortal and The Restless playing in the background, and surreal intermissions that juxtapose normal life with the horrors of the facility. These creative flourishes add a layer of dark comedy and psychological tension, blurring the line between reality and hallucination. They also help flesh out Michael’s character, giving players a deeper emotional anchor than in past games, where the protagonist was often just a blank slate behind a desk.

Visually, Sister Location maintains the franchise’s signature aesthetic—grimy industrial design mixed with unsettlingly lifelike animatronics—but with improved lighting, animations, and atmosphere. The game’s use of darkness and sound is especially effective, forcing players to listen closely and react to subtle audio cues to survive. The character designs for new animatronics like Circus Baby, Funtime Freddy, Ballora, and Ennard strike a balance between whimsical and horrifying, with mechanical details that make them feel simultaneously lifeless and threatening.

There’s also a layer of replayability and hidden content that rewards curious players. A custom night mode, multiple endings, secret mini-games, and hidden audio logs give players reason to revisit the game after completing the main story. These secrets are often cryptic, requiring community collaboration or obsessive exploration to uncover, which is very much in line with the FNaF tradition of hidden lore and community-driven theory crafting.

However, Sister Location is not without its drawbacks. The variance in gameplay mechanics means some sections feel less refined than others, and the heavy reliance on jump scares remains a staple that might feel predictable to horror veterans. There are moments where scripted events overshadow player agency, which can undercut tension when players feel they’re being pushed along a narrow path rather than surviving through their own skill. Still, the atmosphere and storytelling usually manage to maintain a consistent sense of dread and suspense throughout.

In conclusion, Five Nights at Freddy’s: Sister Location is a bold and experimental entry that successfully revitalizes the series by breaking away from its rigid structure. With its diverse gameplay mechanics, expanded lore, improved production values, and narrative ambition, it offers a richer and more theatrical horror experience. It may not appeal equally to all fans—especially those who preferred the simplicity and routine-driven fear of the earlier games—but for those open to evolution within the series, Sister Location stands out as one of the most creative and impactful installments in the FNaF franchise.

Rating: 9/10

The most direct storytelling we’ve had, hearing voices for the first time, and the Funtimes. A great addition to the franchise.

Playing through the nights is fun and the gameplay is varied. There’s a lot of tension but some funny moments too thanks to HandUnit. The springlock minigame is pure pain. The Baby minigame drove me to insanity, but beating it was worth it.

This doesn’t feel like a FNAF game, until you get to Night 5’s fake ending. The custom night is overwhelming but fun, and it’s nice to see the funtime animatronics actually in action since we barely see or hear Ballora and Funtime Foxy in the main game.

I love the Funtime Animatronics’ designs. The way their endoskeletons are tentacle-like makes them seem weirdly alive and makes me so uncomfortable. Ennard. Funtime Freddy’s voice is perfect, I love that silly guy. Baby’s voice is also amazing. All of the voices are great to be honest! Too bad we don’t hear Funtime Foxy during the main game.

The lore here is great (and necessary after the mess FNAF 4 made). The secret blueprints showing the FNAF 4 house layout though, that was cool. “Cancelled due to leaks” - I remember this teaser from Scottgames and thinking that the game was actually cancelled. The tragedy of Michael and the origin of Elizabeth. The return of William. “I’m going to come find you…” still haunts my memories. Decay and purple.

The most unique FNAF game. Nights 1-5 don’t offer much replay value, but the lore is great. And once you get to the fake ending and custom night there’s classic FNAF fun to be had. 9/10

201441660.review: Five Nights At Freddy's Sister Location is ranked as some people's least favourite in the series because it is different to the other games, but I think this slightly adds to the game. In this game you work five nights at a sister location to the Freddy's restaurant called Circus Baby's Entertainment and Rental. Unlike the other games in the series Sister Location has a heavy emphasis on story. I know the other games do have a story but it is not really clear while you are playing. I personally think the story in Sister Location is really good. It's dark, sad and scary, everything I expect from a good horror story. The game play can either be seen as a step up or step down from older titles. Instead of being stuck in the same room for the entire game like in previous titles, each of the five nights take place in different areas of the sister location. Some nights you are checking the animatronics and shocking them if they are not on their stage and some nights you are in the parts and service room repairing the animatronics. Being able to explore the building definitely made me enjoy the main story more. For all the people who like the normal office game play, there is a way to experience that too by completing the secret boss fight on night five and acquiring the secret ending. Once the secret ending is completed you now have access to the custom night mode where you fend off various animatronics from your office chair. I personally believe that this game is a 7/10. I gave the game this score because the main story is good and the custom nights are a great add on to the good story mode. I took 3 points off though since the main story is not the most replayable and night 4 was overly challenging lol.
